blue_origin.event: Blue Origin New Glenn pad explosion
blue_origin.best_public_result: chemical-energy ceiling, not measured blast yield

non_nuclear.name: Mount Tambora 1815 eruption
non_nuclear.basis: Commonly cited total eruption energy: 33 gigatons TNT

non_nuclear.name: Tunguska 1908 airburst
non_nuclear.basis: NASA workshop consensus range: 10 to 20 megatons TNT

non_nuclear.name: Chelyabinsk 2013 airburst
non_nuclear.basis: NASA impact energy estimate: about 440 kt; models 400 to 500 kt

non_nuclear.name: Blue Origin New Glenn pad explosion
non_nuclear.basis: Public full-stack chemical-energy ceiling; actual blast yield is not public

non_nuclear.name: Minor Scale test 1985
non_nuclear.basis: 4,744 tons ANFO; about 4 kt TNT, 17 TJ

non_nuclear.name: Heligoland Operation Big Bang 1947
non_nuclear.basis: Ordnance disposal estimate: about 3.2 kt TNT

non_nuclear.name: Halifax Explosion 1917
non_nuclear.basis: High-explosive cargo detonation: about 2.9 kt TNT

non_nuclear.name: Texas City disaster 1947
non_nuclear.basis: Ammonium nitrate estimates vary from about 1.1 to 3 kt TNT

non_nuclear.name: Port Chicago disaster 1944
non_nuclear.basis: Military ammunition estimate: 1.6 to 2.2 kt TNT

non_nuclear.name: Oppau explosion 1921
non_nuclear.basis: Fertilizer detonation estimate: 1 to 2 kt TNT

non_nuclear.name: Beirut port explosion 2020
non_nuclear.basis: Open waveform and remote sensing range: 0.13 to 2 kt TNT

non_nuclear.name: N1 launch explosion 1969
non_nuclear.basis: Actual blast likely far below 6.93 kt stored propellant energy
